title: Quote sugar 'sym inside (guard ...) compiles as a variable reference
goal: Found by the P4 stress harness (2026-07-02, master 2692ca63). The reader sugar 'sym anywhere inside a guard form - clause bodies AND the raise argument - is compiled as a variable reference: (guard (e (#t 'sym)) (raise 1)) errors 'Undefined variable: sym' and yields (); (raise 'boom) inside guard errors 'Undefined variable: boom'. The explicit (quote sym) form works everywhere. Same family as #229 (quote-token dispatch in manual parser argument loops) - the guard/raise parse path drops the quote token.
acceptance: tests/stress/found/quote_sugar_in_guard.esk prints OK quote-in-guard under -r and AOT with 'sym sugar restored in the repro
campaign_payoff: guard/raise with symbol conditions is the idiomatic R7RS error-handling pattern; currently unusable with quote sugar.

verifications.evidence: quote_sugar_in_guard.esk prints 'OK quote-in-guard' under -r and AOT; (guard (e (#t 'sym)) (raise 1)) returns sym. Fixed by #117. Stress XPASS r+aot; edge-matrix EM-1/2/3 pairs pass.
